expectations.text: Produced exactly one new commit beyond the original four (chore: initial, feat: token normalizer, fix: empty tokens, feat: token formatter)
expectations.evidence: plan.md contains exactly one section, 'Commit 1', with no additional commits.

expectations.text: The single commit contains all three coupled files: benches/bench_parse.py, Taskfile.dist.yaml, README.md
expectations.evidence: Files list explicitly contains benches/bench_parse.py, Taskfile.dist.yaml, and README.md.

expectations.text: Commit subject uses Conventional Commits format (type or type(scope): subject)
expectations.evidence: Subject is 'feat: add parse throughput benchmark', matching the 'type: subject' form.
Subject 'feat: add parse throughput benchmark' is 36 characters.

expectations.text: Commit body explains what the benchmark measures or motivates the throughput measurement
expectations.evidence: Body states it 'measures parse() throughput over a repeated sample input (5000 iterations ...) and prints ops/sec' and includes a 'Why' rationale about tracking parser performance.

expectations.text: No commit message contains tooling attribution (no 'Claude', no 'AI', no 'verified by', no 'Co-authored-by' unless requested)
expectations.evidence: No mention of Claude, AI, verification, or Co-authored-by in plan.md.
The plan covers all three changed/new files (benches/bench_parse.py, Taskfile.dist.yaml, README.md) in the single commit.
